summary: Obfuscated remote-payload dropper executes attacker-controlled binary on require()
details: index.js is the package's main entry and is wrapped in obfuscator.io-style RC4+base64 string-array obfuscation hiding all identifiers, URLs, and command strings. On require(), the deobfuscated code constructs an IP-based URL via four chained.replace() calls (split-string evasion of static URL detection), performs an HTTP GET to that host, writes the response body to a file under os.homedir() with flag 'w+', and then invokes child_process.exec on the dropped file with windowsHide:true and cwd set to the user's home directory. Errors are silenced. The README documents `const mod = require('@array-util/nodepull')` as the only usage, so any consumer of the package triggers the download-and-execute chain immediately. Package metadata is a placeholder (empty description, empty author, ISC license, stub README) consistent with a throwaway dropper publication rather than a legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
